title: Software Engineer, Payments and Risk
description: Note: if you are an intern, new grad, or staff applicant, please do not apply using this link and visit our jobs page for those specific postings. Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies \- from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups \- use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Organization** The Payments organization focuses on developing products and platforms that enable users to accept payments from customers efficiently. This includes building APIs for processing payments, enabling regional, non\-card payment options, and extending Stripe's capabilities to make it easy for businesses to accept in\-person payments. The Risk Engineering team develops products that minimize financial and regulatory risks while ensuring a seamless user experience, thereby safeguarding Stripe’s brand and financial stability. Team Matching: exact team matching for one of the subteams within this org will begin during final stages. Please note we may also consider you for different orgs based on your experience, location, etc. More information on our team matching process can be found here. What you’ll do We’re looking for software engineers who are interested in building software services and platforms that impact thousands of employees and millions of Stripe users, regardless of whether they’re an end user, developer, or partner. **Responsibilities** * Work with a wide range of systems, processes and technologies to own and solve problems from end\-to\-end. * Work closely with engineers, designers, and product managers to build new features and products. * Uphold our high engineering standards and bring consistency to the many codebases and processes you will encounter. * Build elegant APIs and user experiences that enable merchants to run and scale their businesses on top of Stripe. * Contribute to the design and architecture of the next generation of Stripe’s infrastructure, to meet the high growth needs of our company and customers for years to come. Who you are We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * 2\-12\+ years of industry software engineering experience (does not include internships nor includes co\-ops) * You can work effectively across the stack from top to bottom, comfortably navigating codebases with different languages and tools. * You’re equally as comfortable shipping user\-facing experiences as you are writing custom queries to analyze experiment results. * Strong coding skills in any programming language (we understand new languages can be learned on the job so our interview process is language agnostic) * Strong collaboration skills, can work across workstreams within your team and contribute to your peers’ success * Have the ability to thrive on a high level of autonomy, responsibility, and think of yourself as entrepreneurial * Interest in working as a generalist across varying technologies and stacks to solve problems and delight both internal and external users **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience working in high\-growth teams similar to Stripe. * Experience building financial systems or tools for financial experts. In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €85,000 \- €159,600\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Solution Architect
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. Technology at Primark What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. What You’ll Do as a Solution Architect In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Design End\-to\-End Solutions: Lead the design of technical solutions that meet business, functional, and non\-functional requirements, ensuring alignment with enterprise architecture and domain strategy. * Architectural Governance: Oversee compliance with design standards, guardrails, and principles throughout delivery; present and defend designs at architectural forums. * Documentation \& Knowledge Management: Create and maintain solution blueprints, key design decisions, reusable patterns, and technology choices, ensuring critical knowledge is retained and shared. * Technical Leadership Across Lifecycle: Provide direction and assurance from initial budgeting and vendor proposals through to detailed design and delivery oversight, ensuring cost\-effective and accurate implementation. * Stakeholder Engagement \& Collaboration: Work closely with product owners, domain architects, engineering teams, and external partners to shape solution options, evaluate trade\-offs, and ensure clarity and alignment. * Risk \& Roadmap Contribution: Identify technical risks, support mitigation planning, contribute to technology roadmaps, and ensure operational readiness and supportability post\-delivery. What You’ll Get People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. What You’ll Bring Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Experience with UK or global retailers, including PoS deployment and retail payment solutions. * Strong knowledge of Microsoft technologies: Azure, Intune, Defender, and Azure Integration Services. * Solid infrastructure background, including networking, firewalls, and client operating systems. * Previous experience working in high‑transaction processing environments. * Ability to explain technical concepts in clear, business\-friendly language. * Skilled at translating business requirements into effective technical solutions. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-3367

title: Solutions Architect
description: **Senior Solution Architect** **\- Contract \- Dublin** We are looking for a talented senior Solutions Architect to work in the Risk Technology team. The candidate should be interested in designing mission critical applications **As a Solutions Architect your main responsibilities will involve:** * Responsible for designing and documenting solutions to help solve complex business problems * Define and enforce architecture standards, coding best practices, and DevOps/CI\-CD pipelines. * Hands\-on development of PoCs/R\&D initiatives to validate technical feasibility and drive innovation. * Partner with risk, compliance, and engineering teams to translate business needs into technical solutions. * Create UML diagrams, Visio workflows, and clear technical documentation for diverse stakeholders (executives, developers, auditors). * Advise on technology selection, vendor evaluations, and cost\-benefit analyses for risk systems. * Mentor engineering teams on risk\-aware design patterns, cloud security, and resilient architecture. **As Solutions Architect your skills and qualifications will ideally include:** * 5\+ years in solution architecture or equivalent roles * Expertise in microservices/SOA design using containerized environments * Experience with high\-volume ETL/data processing * Experience designing polyglot systems using different database technologies \- RDBMS (SQL Server or PostgreSQL), NOSQL (ideally MongoDB) * Experience with RESTful/GraphQL web APIs and async communication flows using (Redis Streams, Kafka etc.) * Exceptional stakeholder management, diagramming (UML/Visio), and presentation skills. Ability to simplify complex technical concepts for non\-technical audiences * Strong written and oral communication skills **Bonus skills** * Experience with TimeSeries databases (like Prometheus), Graph native databases like Neo4j or TigerGraph etc. * Hands on coding skills in Python * Familiarity with Agile methodology **Berkley Business \& Technology is a specialist consultancy, recruiting professionals for organizations throughout Ireland, UK \& Europe; for more information go to** **www.berkley\-group.com** **.** **If you would like to discuss this vacancy further or to discuss your career options in confidence, send your profile to eryan@berkley\-group.com**

title: Solutions Engineer, EMEA
description: **About Us** Notion helps you build beautiful tools for your life’s work. In today's world of endless apps and tabs, Notion provides one place for teams to get everything done, seamlessly connecting docs, notes, projects, calendar, and email—with AI built in to find answers and automate work. Millions of users, from individuals to large organizations like Toyota, Figma, and OpenAI, love Notion for its flexibility and choose it because it helps them save time and money. In\-person collaboration is essential to Notion's culture. We require all team members to work from our offices on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays, our designated Anchor Days. Certain teams or positions may require additional in\-office workdays. **About The Role** The Notion sales organisation has a strong Product Led Growth lean, with many customers entering our funnel through our self\-service motion before our product grows toward being a wall\-to\-wall solution. As we grow, more and more prospects are investigating how Notion can integrate with their existing tech stack, replace existing solutions, or be deployed in large or phased implementation to support desired business goals. As an early member of the Solutions Engineering team, you will: * Partner with members of the sales org on customer engagements with prospective and existing customers * Respond to advanced product and technical questions, RFPs, and security questionnaires in a precise and timely manner * Provide thoughtful technical guidance to customers throughout the sales process, with a focus on quickly enabling customers to successfully deploy and integrate Notion * Showcase custom solutions with Notion based on deep understanding of Notion’s ability to support customer workflows and integrated applications as part of the pre\-sales process * Curate structured product feedback to enable the Product and Engineering teams to enhance and refine Notion to solve customer needs * Work with marketing to refine our technical narrative for the product, including blog posts, webinars, and customer\-facing marketing collateral * Educate the sales org on the product, upcoming releases, competitive analysis, and topical information about typical customer environments and third party applications * Document technical considerations and contribute to internal knowledge bases as a resource for other teams, customers, and partners **What You'll Achieve** * Serve as the customer's technical advisor: You'll help customers craft Notion to serve their specific business needs and explore new workflows and use cases. * Meaningfully shape our future product and messaging: As you engage with customers, you'll gain insights to help us serve them better — and work with product to inform what we do next. * Help build the foundations of Solutions Engineering at Notion \- As an early member of our SE org and regional team, you'll be instrumental in helping define our sales\-assisted motions. * Join a fantastic team at a magical time: Notion has over three million users with a small team, which gives us a huge green field to work with. You'll join at the perfect time to shape how we grow from here. * Represent Notion at events: You'll be the face of Notion at industry conferences, trade shows, and customer events. You'll present our product vision and showcase its features, helping to build trust and strong relationships with both prospective and existing customers. * Elevate your skills as we tackle our most impactful challenges: We've achieved more than teams 10x our size in less time — and there's so much more to do and learn. **Skills You'll Need To Bring** * 4\+ years as Technical Sales Engineer, Solutions Engineer or Solutions Architect * You’re able to understand and explain how APIs can be used to support advanced customer workflows * You have experience working with third\-party integrations for IAM/SSO, DLP or SIEM platforms * You have strong project management experience and have managed complex projects to completion * You have a growth mindset and thrive in the building stage of a nascent team * You’re highly adaptable, easily acclimating to a rapidly changing business and industry **Nice To Haves** * You've been a first or early hire at a fast\-growing startup before * You have experience using Notion * You have coding experience that would enable you to write basic scripts using the Notion API * You have experience using with and designing relational databases We hire talented and passionate people from a variety of backgrounds because we want our global employee base to represent the wide diversity of our customers. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every bullet point listed in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. If you’re a builder at heart, share our company values, and enthusiastic about making software toolmaking ubiquitous, we want to hear from you. Notion is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or any employment decision based on race, colour, religion, national origin, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. Notion considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Notion is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please let your recruiter know. By clicking “Submit Application”, I understand and agree that Notion and its affiliates and subsidiaries will collect and process my information in accordance with Notion’s Global Recruiting Privacy Policy.

title: Spontaneous Application - Join Us
description: **Why Join Access?** 1. **Fast\-track your career:** Promotions based on performance, not time served 2. **Earn more, sooner:** High achievers increase earnings quickly 3. **Full training provided:** No prior experience needed \- just ambition **It’s time to move into a role where your results actually matter.** At Access, we’re growing across different markets and looking for driven individuals ready to build a career in a fast\-paced, people\-focused environment. If you bring the energy and mindset, we’ll give you the structure, coaching, and platform to succeed. **The Roles:** * **Business Development:** You’ll focus on building and managing client relationships, expanding our client base, and identifying where we can deliver the most value. This role is all about commercial impact and growth. * **Recruitment Consultant:** You’ll focus on people \- sourcing candidates, managing registrations, and placing them into roles with our clients. You act as the link between talent and opportunity. **The difference?** Business Development is client\-focused. Recruitment is candidate\-focused. **What’s the same?** Strong communication, relationship\-building, and a results\-driven mindset. **What We Offer You** * **Clear Career Progression from Day One:** You’ll know exactly how to move into senior and leadership roles, with full transparency at every stage. * **Personalised coaching:** Tailored support based on your unique strengths and development needs. * **Hands\-On Training:** Structured training, real\-time mentoring, and **continuous development built into your day\-to\-day work.** * **Merit\-Based Promotions:** High achievers gain responsibility faster and are promoted sooner \- many team members were **promoted 3–4 times in their first year** . * **Growth from Within:** We promote from within \- your mentors and managers have been in your shoes, so they understand the challenges and are invested in helping you succeed. **Additional Benefits:** * **Ideal City Centre Location –** Central Dublin 2 Office Based (Monday\-Friday) * **Commission \& Bonus –** Paid monthly, completely uncapped * **Lucrative Incentives –** All Inclusive European Trips, Team Events, \& Celebrations * **High\-Performing Team –** Collaborate with High\-Performing Sales Professionals * **Base\+ Uncapped Commission:** Average performers earn €45K\+ in Year 1 and **€75K\+ in Year 2** **Why Stay Where You Are When You Can Step Up?** If you’re ready to build a fast\-moving career in **Business Development or Recruitment** , we want to hear from you. For more information, contact nadia.garcia@accesshealthcare.ie or call \+353 1 572 1159\.

title: Studyclix Schools Team - Edtech Specialist
description: **About Us** Studyclix is Ireland's leading teaching and learning platform for secondary school students and teachers. More than half the schools in Ireland now buy Studyclix access for their students. We're a small, fully remote team that moves quickly, works flexibly, and genuinely enjoys what we do. We're growing our school sales team, and we want someone who can help us reach every school in Ireland. **The Role** This is a field and inside sales role focused on secondary schools in the Dublin and Greater Dublin Region. You'll own the full sales cycle, from first contact to closed deal, and play a key role in keeping existing schools renewing year on year. You'll work alongside a Customer Success Manager and a Sales Fulfilment Specialist, with real support behind you. But you're someone who doesn't need hand\-holding. You can work independently, you love meeting school leaders and teachers, and you know how to build trust with busy school principals. **What You'll Do** ● Generate leads and close new school accounts ● Manage renewals and deepen existing relationships ● Call to schools on pre\-arranged appointments, and also be open to visiting schools without appointment ● Deliver onboarding sessions; online and in person ● Run email campaigns using HubSpot (with support) ● Represent Studyclix at conferences and school events ● Train teachers and students on getting the most from the platform ● Keep CRM records clean and up to date **You're Right For This If You...** ● Have B2B sales experience, ideally SaaS ● Know how Irish secondary schools work (you've either worked in or sold into them) ● Are a natural communicator who can present confidently to a room or on Zoom ● Have strong organisational skills and a keen eye for detail ● Are self\-directed; you don't wait to be told what to do ● Have done the Leaving Cert ● Hold a full driver's licence and are happy to travel across Dublin and beyond occasionally **What's In It For You** ● Fully remote with flexible hours ● Base salary \+ commission; your results are rewarded! ● a tight\-knit team ● The ability to contribute to the product roadmap and influence the tools and content we build ● Real scope to sell a product that schools love **How to Apply** Send your CV to luke@studyclix.ie (\+ a short note on why you'd be great at this goes a long way). Or apply directly via LinkedIn.

title: Supply Chain Planner & Coordinator M/F/D
description: Hager Supply Chain \& Logistics Dublin, Ireland Permanent **Why join the** **Supply Chain \& Logistic** **department?** Our supply chain and logistics teams implement advanced solutions to manage material flows and optimize distribution, ensuring speed and reliability in product delivery and building a competitive infrastructure. We are committed to leading initiatives, taking concrete actions to ensure the smooth delivery of our products to meet and exceed customer expectations. By joining our supply chain and logistics team, you will contribute to the comprehensive management of our logistics chain, from procurement to delivery. You will collaborate with a passionate team dedicated to improving the efficiency and resilience of our operations in a safe environment. You will take pride in utilizing innovative solutions that minimize our environmental footprint and in implementing sustainable practices throughout our operations. In the Supply Chain \& Logistics department, we are shaping tomorrow’s electrical world. You can too, Join us! As a **Supply Chain Planner \& Coordinator M/F/D** you will report to the Planning \& Scheduling Leader in the Supply Chain \& Logistics based in **Dublin** for a **Permanent** contract. **What you will do ?** * **Review, challenge and improve demand forecasts** to ensure alignment with customer needs and production capacity, identifying supply risks early and driving mitigation actions. * **Control inventory and replenishment** by optimising stock levels, safety stocks and reorder points across warehouses, assembly and external locations, in line with Group standards. * **Plan supply and manage gaps** through regular supply analysis, close collaboration with manufacturing sites, and proactive escalation of risks to minimise customer impact. * **Coordinate cross‑functionally** with Sales, Projects, Customer Service and Operations, acting as the key contact for supply issues, backorders and logistics complaints. * **Manage supply processes in SAP and data reporting**, including purchase and intercompany orders, master data accuracy, KPI tracking and Excel‑based reporting. * **Ensure Quality, Health, Safety and Environmental compliance**, applying Company standards, procedures and policies in daily supply chain operations. **What about you ?** * You hold a **third‑level qualification in Supply Chain, Business or a related discipline**, or you bring significant equivalent experience in supply chain, inventory planning or operations within a distribution or manufacturing environment. * You have **proven experience across end‑to‑end supply chain activities**, including demand planning, inventory management and supply coordination. * You demonstrate **strong planning, organisational and analytical skills**, with a high level of attention to detail and data accuracy in a fast‑paced environment. * You are **solution‑oriented and proactive**, able to work on your own initiative, interpret data and support effective decision‑making. * You are comfortable working **cross‑functionally**, building effective and influential relationships with Sales, Operations, Customer Service and Supply Chain stakeholders. * You have **strong IT proficiency**, with essential experience using **SAP** and **advanced Microsoft Excel**. **What we offer?** * Personalized onboarding experience program designed to make your first days smooth * Opportunities to learn and grow through Hager Group's awarded Hi University. * Internal career opportunities, over 25% of our recruitments are through internal mobility **What are the recruitment stages?** * Once your CV has been selected, you will be contacted by a recruiter for an initial phone exchange. * You will be invited for a first interview with the hiring manager \& the recruiter * A second Interview will be then organized with the N\+2 \& the HR manager **Our success relies on collective energy. Apply now and power up your future with us!**

title: Supply Chain Specialist – Food Business
description: The successful candidates will deliver a high level of supply chain excellence in a fast paced, dynamic environment. A key requirement of the role includes creating partnerships with commercial and operations teams to maximize availability to customers and supply chain efficiency. **Experience,** * The successful candidate will have / be: * Previous experience in Inventory Planning essential, ideally in a Fast Food/foodservice environment * Excellent planning and time management skills, with the ability to confidently prioritise tasks * An understanding of forecasting activity and supplier management * Track record in the process development to drive continuous improvement * Proficient in Microsoft Office, specifically Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint * A team player with a high degree of enthusiasm, coupled with the ability to achieve results and adapt to change in a fast\-paced environment. * Third Level Qualification, degree desirable or equivalent business experience **Key duties** * Managing inventory to ensure 100% availability to support customer demand. * Measuring and managing supplier delivery performance through an account manager approach with appropriate regular service levels communication and calls * Identifying and challenging safety stock levels, MOQ’s and supplier lead\-times as part of your inventory management with a focus on providing optimal availability and shelf life to customers. * Managing waste risk on short life product and analysing opportunities to optimise waste management. * Liaising with Trading and Sales colleagues to manage range and inventory ensuring overstocks, residual stocks and waste risks are being addressed * Providing ad hoc inventory analysis and reporting for your portfolio * Supporting Demand Planners with forecasting on standard and promotional activity across all channels (including of anticipation of seasonal trends) * Collaborating closely with warehouse team to achieve maximum efficiency * Communicating product availability/recovery to the wider business * Reviewing operating systems, processes and practices on an ongoing basis, recommending changes to improve operational effectiveness and efficiency to reduce costs * Ensuring the business achieves stock management targets * Supporting new customer and new product launches * Devising, implementing and managing supply chain projects that deliver added value services and cost benefits

title: Systems Administrator
description: My Dublin City Centre based client is recruiting for a Systems Administrator to join a well established IT function supporting a dynamic professional services organisation. The Systems Administrator will play a key role in maintaining and enhancing core infrastructure, cloud platforms, networking, and security systems across the business. Key Skills \& Experience * Proven experience supporting and administering Windows Server environments (2019 or later) across both on\-premise and cloud\-based infrastructures. * Strong working knowledge of Active Directory and Azure Active Directory, including DNS, DHCP, and Group Policy administration. * Hands\-on experience managing Microsoft 365 technologies such as Exchange Online, Intune, Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive. * Experience supporting SQL Server platforms and related database environments. * Exposure to enterprise application platforms and business\-critical systems is desirable. * Good understanding of networking technologies, protocols, and infrastructure support. * Experience managing cybersecurity tools, endpoint protection, patching solutions, and firewall technologies. * Strong troubleshooting and configuration skills across enterprise switching, routing, and wireless technologies. * Experience supporting remote connectivity and VPN solutions. * Familiarity with collaboration and conferencing technologies, including AV and meeting room systems. * Provide escalation support for technical issues across the wider IT function. * Manage competing priorities effectively within a fast\-paced environment. * Contribute to IT improvement initiatives, projects, and operational best practices. Infrastructure Support Provide ongoing administration and support across server, desktop, and network environments. * Monitor infrastructure performance and proactively address issues to minimise disruption. * Work alongside third\-party providers and internal teams to maintain system reliability and service availability. * Assist with system upgrades, deployments, patching, and lifecycle management activities. * Support endpoint deployment and configuration using modern management tools and technologies. Microsoft 365 / Cloud Services * Manage and support Microsoft 365 applications and cloud services across the organisation. * Troubleshoot technical issues and perform routine maintenance activities. * Ensure systems are operating in line with security, compliance, and operational standards. Security / Compliance * Support the implementation and ongoing maintenance of IT security controls and technologies. * Review security alerts, reports, and audit findings, taking appropriate corrective actions where required. * Assist with security reviews, access controls, and infrastructure hardening initiatives. * Coordinate infrastructure and firewall\-related changes as part of ongoing security management.

title: Talent Acquisition Partner, EMEA
description: **Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.** We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. **Job Title** Talent Acquisition Partner, EMEA**Talent Acquisition Partner** **What does a successful Talent Acquisition Advisor do?** A successful Talent Acquisition Partner at Fiserv plays a crucial role in identifying and attracting top talent to join our dynamic and innovative team. They collaborate with hiring managers to understand staffing needs and create strategies to source and hire the best candidates. By living our values and creating a culture of respect, they ensure that we continue to build a diverse and inclusive workforce that drives our mission forward. We pride ourselves on being true partners to our business leaders, while delivering a best\-in\-class candidate experience. Joining this team requires a genuine passion and enthusiasm for all aspects of TA. **What you will do:** * Develop and implement effective recruiting strategies to attract high\-caliber candidates. * Source potential candidates through various channels, including job boards, social media, and professional networks. * Screen resumes and applications to identify qualified candidates. * Conduct initial interviews to assess candidates' suitability and fit for the company culture. * Collaborate with hiring managers to understand their staffing needs and provide guidance throughout the hiring process. * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date candidate information in the applicant tracking system. * Provide an exceptional candidate experience, ensuring timely communication and feedback. * Participate in job fairs, career events, and other recruiting activities to promote the Fiserv brand. **What you will need to have:** * A love and passion for all elements of recruitment * Strong knowledge of recruitment processes and best practices. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to build and maintain relationships with candidates and hiring managers. * Proficiency with applicant tracking systems and recruiting tools. * Strong organizational and time management skills. **What would be great to have:** * Previous experience in the fintech industry. * Experience with recruitment marketing and employer branding. * Knowledge of diversity and inclusion best practices. * Certification in Talent Acquisition or Human Resources * Bilingual capabilities. Thank you for considering employment with Fiserv. Please: * Apply using your legal name * Complete the step\-by\-step profile and attach your resume (either is acceptable, both are preferable). **Our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:** Fiserv is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by law. **Note to agencies:** Fiserv does not accept resume submissions from agencies outside of existing agreements. Please do not send resumes to Fiserv associates. Fiserv is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited resume submissions. **Warning about fake job posts:** Please be aware of fraudulent job postings that are not affiliated with Fiserv. Fraudulent job postings may be used by cyber criminals to target your personally identifiable information and/or to steal money or financial information. Any communications from a Fiserv representative will come from a legitimate Fiserv email address.

title: Tech, Operations
description: **Job Description** A fantastic and exciting opportunity has arisen for a **Manufacturing Biotech Associate** . This role will involve working shift, on a 24/7 basis. This role is a site based position. The Manufacturing BioTech Associate will work in a manufacturing self\-directed shift team to deliver batch operations successfully, identify and resolve issues in order to allow the site to deliver for our patients in a culture that supports Quality, EHS, Learning and Continuous improvement. Our facility in Dunboyne joined our network in September 2020\. Our Dunboyne site is our Company’s first Biologics Drug Substance Single Use Commercialisation Facility, playing an important role in helping to commercialise key medicines. The cutting\-edge innovation and collaboration between our Company’s research and manufacturing teams will significantly accelerate the time it takes to bring a medicine to market for the benefit of patients around the world. **Bring energy, knowledge, innovation to carry out the following** * The production of biologic drug substance on their designated shift team and will be responsible for the preparation, operation and monitoring of equipment, use of business systems and carrying out tasks including but not limited to the cleaning, validation, in\-process testing. * The progression of an automated recipe using paperless technology in order to generate the drug substance through inoculation and cell culturing activities directing to filtration and purification of the product. * Setting up the single use equipment between runs, carrying out the production process according to world class GMP and EHS standards and driving to optimise and improve the processing activities through use of standard work and lean processing techniques. * Focusing on delivery to the patient through living the Dunboyne culture including a commitment to safety, focusing on right first time in everything we do and concentrating on the engagement and development of self and peers. **What Skills You Will Need** In order to excel in this role, you will more than likely have * A science qualification in a technical, engineering or science\-based discipline or equivalent. * 0 to 3 years’ experience in the biotechnology and/or pharmaceutical industry or equivalent. * Experience in a highly regulated pharmaceutical manufacturing environment. * Good understanding in the production of biologic drug substance and experience an advantage. * Ability to work as part of a shift team and on own initiative in a constructive manner. * Ability to think logically and be proactive under pressure. * Flexible and self\-motivated. We keep the patient at the very heart of all that we do and strive to find solutions and treatments for some of the world's most challenging healthcare needs. As an equal opportunity employer, we are proud to be a company that embraces the value of bringing diverse, talented, and committed people together. Please don't hesitate to contact the Talent Acquisition Advisor assigned to this role should you need any support during our recruitment process. We are committed to flexible working where possible, and this is why our Company has introduced a new hybrid working model for office\-based colleagues around the world. Please feel free to speak to us about what flexibility means to you during your application. **So, if you are ready to** Invent solutions to meet unmet healthcare needs, **please apply today.** **Required Skills** Analytical Problem Solving, Analytical Problem Solving, Cell Cultures, Computer Literacy, Corporate Productions, Data Entry, Equipment Maintenance, GMP Compliance, GMP Documentation, Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), Key Performance Indicators (KPI), Manufacturing Compliance, Manufacturing Documentation, Manufacturing Operations, Manufacturing Processes, Manufacturing Quality Control, Media Preparation, Motivating Teams, Organizational Structures, Process Improvements, Process Manufacturing, Production Processes, Quality Control Management, Regulatory Compliance, Safety Practices {\+ 2 more} **Preferred Skills** Current Employees apply HERE Current Contingent Workers apply HERE **Search Firm Representatives Please Read Carefully** Merck \& Co., Inc., Rahway, NJ, USA, also known as Merck Sharp \& Dohme LLC, Rahway, NJ, USA, does not accept unsolicited assistance from search firms for employment opportunities. All CVs / resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at our company without a valid written search agreement in place for this position will be deemed the sole property of our company. No fee will be paid in the event a candidate is hired by our company as a result of an agency referral where no pre\-existing agreement is in place. Where agency agreements are in place, introductions are position specific. Please, no phone calls or emails. **Employee Status** Regular **Relocation** **VISA Sponsorship** **Travel Requirements** **Flexible Work Arrangements** Not Applicable **Shift** **Valid Driving License** **Hazardous Material(s)** **Job Posting End Date** 05/29/2026 * A job posting is effective until 11 59 59PM on the day BEFORE the listed job posting end date. Please ensure you apply to a job posting no later than the day BEFORE the job posting end date. **Requisition ID** R397548

title: Technical Support Engineer
description: **Who is Tenable?** Tenable® is the Exposure Management company. 44,000 organizations around the globe rely on Tenable to understand and reduce cyber risk. Our global employees support 65 percent of the Fortune 500, 45 percent of the Global 2000, and large government agencies. Come be part of our journey! **What makes Tenable such a great place to work?** Ask a member of our team and they’ll answer, “Our people!” We work together to build and innovate best\-in\-class cybersecurity solutions for our customers; all while creating a culture of belonging, respect, and excellence where we can be our best selves. When you’re part of our \#OneTenable team, you can expect to partner with some of the most talented and passionate people in the industry, and have the support and resources you need to do work that truly matters. We deliver results that exceed expectations and we win together! **Your Role** Join our Technical Support Team at Tenable where you’ll play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between our products and customers. Solve real\-world technical challenges by offering exceptional support for cutting\-edge vulnerability assessment and compliance auditing software. Your expertise will shine in complex, multi\-layered customer environments. Our suite of products, detailed at https://www.tenable.com/products, offers a diverse range of opportunities. From assisting customers with their first Nessus scanner to guiding them through intricate log correlation and real\-time vulnerability analysis, you'll contribute to mitigating cyber risks and closing the Cyber Exposure gap. Join us in delivering top\-notch support that makes a difference! **Your Opportunity** * Serves as the primary technical liaison between the customer and Tenable internal groups (Product Management, Sales, R\&D, etc) for technical issues. * Analyse and clarify customer technical inquiries. * Analysing vulnerability scan results, system audits, and log events. * Recreating customer software issues in a lab environment. * Harness AI\-driven technologies to amplify your impact, automate routine tasks, and focus on solving complex technical challenges. * Maintaining in\-depth knowledge of Tenable products and information security best\-practices. * Create and publish solution knowledge for re\-use by customers and Tenable employees. **What You'll Need** * Bachelor’s degree in IT or Computer Science (or equivalent experience). * 2\+ years professional experience with networking (TCP/IP), OS administration(Linux/Windows/MacOS), patch deployment, and system configuration. * Experience providing technical support to enterprise clients, troubleshooting complex issues in production or customer environments; network security experience a plus. * Familiarity with Artificial Intelligence (AI) as Tenable continues to build an AI\-augmented support model to accelerate case resolutions and to provide an exceptional customer/TSE experience. * Knowledge of computer, network, and application security; familiarity with Nessus or similar tools is a bonus. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to learn quickly. * Excellent written and verbal communication; customer\-focused mindset. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment to help meet business and client needs. * Monthly availability to work weekends (8am\-4:30pm) and Holidays is also required. **And Ideally** * Using Nessus and/or other vulnerability management or cybersecurity tools, either professionally or in an academic setting. * Experience with ticketing systems (JIRA, Salesforce, Zendesk etc). * Log analysis using a SEIM product (Splunk, ElasticSearch, etc). * Database technologies and SQL knowledge (Oracle, MySQL, MSSQL, etc). * Cloud/Virtualization technologies (AWS, Azure, Docker, Kubernetes, etc). * Fundamental understanding of programming languages. * Experience with or understanding of Certificates and CA’s. * Basic understanding of reviewing PCAPs with tools such as Wireshark. * Bilingual candidates are desirable but not required (Arabic/French/German/Spanish/Italian). *We’re committed to promoting Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) at Tenable \- through all equal employment opportunity laws and regulations at the international, federal, state and local levels. If you need a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please contact* *Recruiting@Tenable.com* *for further assistance.* **Tenable Data Consent Statement** Tenable is committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal data. This Notice describes how we collect and use your personal data during and after your working relationship with us, in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (“GDPR”). Please click here to review. For California Residents: The California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) requires that Tenable advise you of certain rights related to the collection of your private information. Please click here to review.

title: Technology Lawyer
description: The market\-leading Technology and Innovation group in Matheson LLP (" **Matheson** ") is seeking to build on its ongoing growth and success. The group, which is the longest established technology practice in Ireland, is acknowledged to be at the cutting edge of technology related legal developments. The group advises on all aspects of technology related legal advice, providing strategic, practical and commercially focused advice to international and domestic clients across multiple sectors. The work includes advising on digital services and content regulation, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, data protection and privacy matters, intellectual property and strategic and business critical contracts, both transactional and advisory. The group has grown substantially over the last several years and is recruiting ambitious senior associates who are keen to develop their expertise and help manage further continuing growth. **The role** This is a senior role within the Technology and Innovation group, where you will: * Lead on challenging and innovative technology law projects for a variety of clients; * Work collaboratively as part of multi\-disciplinary specialist teams (including Matheson’s Digital Economy Group) on high value and business critical client projects; * Manage junior and associate solicitors on client advisory matters across the full range of technology, digital services and privacy issues; * Participate in a variety of business development opportunities including foreign client relationship visits and representing Matheson at international conferences as well as the possibility of secondment to our London and Californian offices; * Help drive thought leadership in technology law through the Matheson client knowledge programme; and Advise on the legal issues across: * Large scale technology and commercial contract drafting and negotiations; * Data protection, data privacy and data governance; * Artificial intelligence; * Cyber security and operational resilience; * The digital single market including DMA and DSA compliance, content regulation for online platforms and intermediary industries, e\-commerce and online business practices for gatekeepers, platforms and businesses; * Transactional and advisory intellectual property projects. **The person** * You are an experienced Technology Lawyer with a strong track record both technically and in business development. * You are highly client focused and committed to consistently exceeding client expectations. * You have a strong academic track record and broad technical expertise. * You can manage a diverse workload, deliver quality, commercially\-focused advice with minimal supervision, and lead and develop junior lawyers. * You enjoy the law you practice, and are hardworking and resilient. You will thrive in a diverse and inclusive environment where you will work in partnership with top legal talent in a market leading team that is continuing to grow. * You will enjoy establishing relationships with a diverse range of clients and colleagues. * You are enthusiastic about business development, helping develop your junior colleagues to their maximum potential and how you can contribute to managing valuable client relationships as part of the team. **Why join Matheson** At Matheson you have the opportunity to work with exceptional people and a broad range of high calibre domestic and international clients. Working at Matheson provides you with an environment where you can thrive by living our values of Partnership, Respect, Innovation, Diversity and Entrepreneurship. Our Impactful Business Programme is delivered by a number of committees which drive key initiatives around D\&I, CSR, Environmental Sustainability and the Arts. We are also a founding signatory of the Pro Bono Pledge Ireland. We have a hybrid working policy. We provide an excellent working environment, a competitive benefits package and the opportunity to engage in a range of learning and development initiatives which are specifically tailored to your level. We are proud to support the Mindful Business Charter. *Accessibility: If you require any accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact us and we will work with you to ensure the best possible candidate experience.* *We value a diverse and inclusive workforce and are an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Telecoms OSP Engineer
description: **Company Description** Novegen is a trusted leader in Telecommunications Infrastructure Management and Independent Certification Solutions. We provide a wide range of services, including telecom asset mapping, network data management, open access network management, and fibre network testing. We help organisations optimise and scale their connectivity infrastructure. Committed to innovation, Novegen partners with clients to build the future of connectivity together. **Role Description** If you’re looking for the opportunity to flex your creative skills within your Engineering qualification, Novegen is providing the chance for you to not only take responsibility for project development with our Engineering team, but also work with internal stakeholders to design and build carrier\-class, access and last\-mile network infrastructure. Rather than just inputting data, a driven, skilled engineer can propose, develop, and implement new automated systems and processes within the project team. **What will you be doing?** * The successful graduate will work closely with the Engineering and GIS Managers who will provide them with regular and objective feedback, valuable insight and support. * Working with internal \& external design \& construction teams to design, deploy, QC \& record a carrier class network infrastructure. * You will need to be proactive and precise with the completion of project documentation. * Offering your advice and guidance to the project team along with data input onto the GIS system. * Be a member of our QA/QC team in relation to network certification. * Preparation and review of preliminary designs and reports using a variety of computer packages and subsequent preparation of detailed design drawings and specifications. * Preparation of Preliminary and Contract Drawings using a range of packages such as AutoCAD 2D/3D and ArcGIS. * Procurement of Site Investigation, Civil Engineering Works and technical contracts including preparation of pricing schedules and Bills of Quantities. * Administration of the construction contracts to ensure that design requirements, project schedules and project budget are tracked and monitored. **What tools will you be using to do this?** * AutoCAD, GIS, DXF, KMZ, KML, MapInfo, Fibre Mapping, Network Planning, Network Design, GIS Diagrams, Fibre Network Schematics. title: Test Analyst
description: We are seeking an experienced IT Test Analyst to join a dynamic technology team responsible for delivering high\-quality digital solutions. The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring that new systems, enhancements, and integrations meet defined business and technical standards. This is a hands\-on testing role requiring strong analytical ability, a structured approach to quality assurance, and a commitment to excellence. **Role Purpose** The IT Test Analyst will be responsible for planning, executing, and coordinating all phases of software testing across multiple projects. This includes developing test strategies, designing test cases, managing test environments, and collaborating closely with development and business teams to ensure successful delivery. **Core Responsibilities** * Analyse business and technical requirements to understand system functionality and testing needs. * Design and maintain comprehensive test plans, scripts, and scenarios to validate functionality, usability, performance, and security. * Conduct manual and automated testing activities to verify system behaviour and data integrity. * Manage test environments, datasets, and configurations to support accurate and repeatable test execution. * Identify, log, and track software defects through to resolution using appropriate tools and processes. * Provide clear, concise reporting on test outcomes, risks, and quality metrics to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with developers, analysts, and project managers to resolve issues and enhance solution quality. * Support the continuous improvement of testing processes, tools, and documentation standards. **Experience \& Qualifications** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a software testing or QA role within a technology\-focused environment. * Relevant third\-level qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. * Proven experience testing web, cloud, or enterprise applications. * Strong knowledge of software testing principles, tools, and methodologies. * Understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and common delivery approaches such as Agile or Waterfall. * Experience with defect management and test management tools (e.g., JIRA, Azure DevOps, TestRail). * Ability to create and maintain traceability between requirements, test cases, and results. * Excellent analytical, documentation, and communication skills. * Strong attention to detail and a commitment to producing high\-quality work. * Ability to work independently and contribute effectively within a team setting. **Desirable Attributes** * Professional certifications in software testing or quality assurance (e.g., ISTQB Foundation Level or equivalent). * Experience with test automation frameworks or scripting languages. * Exposure to API, integration, or performance testing. * Familiarity with DevOps and CI/CD environments. * Demonstrated initiative, curiosity, and a proactive approach to learning new tools and technologies. **Personal Qualities** * A structured thinker who enjoys problem\-solving and continuous improvement. * Excellent communicator who can translate technical concepts for non\-technical stakeholders. * Reliable, accountable, and focused on delivering results. * Adaptable and comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with changing priorities.

title: Training Specialist
description: **DON’T BE IN THE DARK ABOUT YOUR CAREER…….** **WE WANT YOU TO HELP US CREATE THE DIGITAL FUTURE OF OUR COMPANY** We are looking for exceptional people to join our diverse and multi\-cultural team. This is a fantastic opportunity to play a lead role as we connect process, data and technology, creating sustainable business benefits and drive the achievement of Divisional, Sales Area and Common Function business outcomes. As we are creating a new operating model, this presents a great opportunity for learning and career development in this full\-time position. **We are now recruiting for a Training Specialist – (Hybrid), a permanent role, based in Dublin, Ireland.** This is a role reporting to the Head of Change Management and Training. The organization seeks to drive performance excellence through process improvement and delivering on desired business outcomes across all E2E processes. **JOB PURPOSE** The Training Specialist is a key role within the Change Management and Training team, supporting global process, system, AI, and digital transformation initiatives through role\-based learning and enablement activities. Responsibilities include supporting the design, development, coordination, and continuous improvement of learning solutions aligned to approved business processes and ways of working. Working closely with the Training Lead, Change Management Lead and process teams, the Training Specialist helps employees build confidence in new processes, systems, and ways of working to support operational readiness and successful adoption. This role contributes to business adoption through practical, learner\-focused enablement activities and learning support across transformation and continuous improvement initiatives. **Main Responsibilities** * **Design and develop enablement materials:** Create role\-based learning and support materials including e\-learning, job aids, workflow guides, facilitator guides, and learner support materials based on approved business processes and aligned to agreed learning standards and governance requirements. * **Plan and coordinate learning interventions:** Coordinate training schedules, logistics, communications, and learner administration for virtual and instructor\-led sessions. Support instructors and delivery teams to ensure learning activities run smoothly across global audiences. * **Support training needs analysis and planning:** Work with process teams and the Training Lead to support the identification of role\-based learning needs and recommended enablement approaches. * **Support super\-user enablement activities:** Assist with onboarding materials, communications, and learning support activities for super\-users and local business support networks. * **Contribute to readiness and adoption activities:** Support learning readiness activities, track training and adoption metrics, and help identify improvement actions where required. * **Collaborate across process and program teams:** Partner with process teams and subject matter experts to develop practical learning materials and support a consistent user adoption approach. * Other Ad hoc Duties as requested by management. **Experience** * Experience supporting learning and enablement activities within transformation or technology implementation environments. * Experience developing digital learning, enablement, or communication materials. * Strong interpersonal and stakeholder coordination skills across global teams. * Ability to create visually clear and user\-friendly learning and communication materials. * Experience supporting training needs analysis and role\-based learning planning. * Organized and structured approach to managing priorities and stakeholder relationships. * Comfortable working across cultures, time zones, and fast\-paced delivery environments. Essential: * Minimum 3 years’ experience supporting learning or training activities within transformation or technology\-enabled environments. * Understanding of instructional design principles and learner\-focused content development. * Experience developing learner\-focused training and enablement materials. * Experience supporting training needs analysis and role\-based learning planning. * Strong written communication skills with the ability to produce clear and audience\-appropriate materials. Equality of opportunity is fully supported and endorsed as an employer by Sandvik, while also welcoming the additional strength that diversity brings, by providing a work environment where everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. **Sandvik Mining and Rock Solutions** Mining is a business area within the Sandvik Group and a global leading supplier of equipment and tools, parts, service, digital solutions and sustainability\-driving technologies for the mining and construction industries. Application areas include rock drilling, rock cutting, loading and hauling, tunneling and quarrying. title: Transformation Change Analyst - Customer Availability
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **Transformation Change Analyst \- Customer Availability** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Support delivery of change initiatives at project/workstream level, working across multiple projects within the same functional area, contributing to impact analysis, stakeholder engagement, communications, training, and readiness activities * Conduct change impact assessments, stakeholder mapping, and data gathering, while supporting the preparation of communications, training content, and engagement materials to guide colleagues through change * Coordinate stakeholder sessions, workshops, and feedback loops, ensuring effective engagement and capturing insights on risks, resistance, and adoption themes * Assist with readiness assessments and adoption tracking, providing meaningful insights into progress, risks, and overall organisational readiness * Support execution of project\-level change activities and timelines, including go\-lives, hypercare, and surveys, while maintaining accurate documentation, reporting, and tracking * Collaborate closely with Change Managers/Leads, SMEs, and impacted teams to deliver a consistent experience, build strong relationships, and escalate risks, resistance, or key insights promptly. **What You’ll Get** This role is a hybrid opportunity, offering 2\-3 days Working from office. People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Degree (or equivalent experience) with 1–3\+ years’ experience in Change, Transformation, PMO, Communications, HR, or related roles, including supporting project or change delivery activities * Familiarity with core change management concepts (impacts, stakeholders, training, communications) and experience preparing materials, documentation, and internal reports * Exposure (nice to have) to large transformations, technology deployments, or operational change, ideally within retail, FMCG, or other fast\-paced environments, along with experience in survey tools, data gathering, or basic analytics and an interest in Change Management (certification optional) * Strong organisation, coordination, and written communication skills, with the ability to create clear materials, interpret impacts, risks, and feedback, and maintain strong attention to detail * Ability to use data to generate insights (Excel, dashboards, survey tools), work with collaboration tools (Teams, Miro, etc.), build positive stakeholder relationships, and support reporting, workshop facilitation, and basic design (nice to have) Curious, proactive, and eager\-to\-learn team player who is comfortable working in ambiguous, fast\-changing environments, and is energetic, reliable, and delivery\-focused * Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **\#LI\-Hybrid** **REQ ID:** JR\-8881

title: Travel Product Executive – Full time / permanent role.
description: Travel Department is Ireland’s leading escorted tour operator with an approach to business that is innovative, dynamic and professional. We have an unsurpassed reputation within our market for excellent customer service, quality holidays and value. For over 28 years, our team of experts have been creating fully guided group holidays with best\-in\-class service and an ever\-growing product portfolio. We have an exciting opportunity for a Product Executive. Are you a person with a flair for building travel itineraries and a passion for travel? If so, we just might have the perfect role for you! Our Product Executives work closely with our suppliers throughout the world to build and plan the best itineraries to suit our very valued customers. Do you have an outgoing personality with a commercial focus and strong negotiation skills? Previous travel industry experience is preferable. You would report directly to our Product Manager. **You will be responsible for:** * Travel Department products. * Booking and administering services with inbound operators, hotels, coach companies and guides. * Creating website content for all elements of the tour including itinerary and hotel descriptions. * Updating Travel Department’s website with product information with relevant SEO content. * Proofing and checking reader offers, newspaper inserts, brochures and other marketing material. * Providing appropriate reports to suppliers based on allocations and customer feedback. * Monitoring success of tours and responding effectively. * Assisting with developing new tour types and new destinations. * Updating / training reservations staff with product details. * Researching and developing new and interesting itineraries including new coaching itineraries and holidays to new destinations. * Occasional site visits for hotel \& destination inspections. * Review weekly trading reports and analyze data to report back to management with suggested actions. * Collaborating closely with all teams across the company to achieve the goal of TD successfully living its values. **We would like you to have:** * Very strong attention to detail. * Team working skills. * A commercial focus and strong negotiation skills. * An outgoing personality with the ability to manage relationships with people and suppliers worldwide. * The ability to learn quickly in a busy environment. * Flexibility \- ability to multitask. * Work well under pressure and to tight deadlines. * An excellent knowledge of Word / Excel. **Salary / package details for the right candidate:** * Full\-time permanent role. * Working days, Monday to Friday. * 20 days annual leave per year. * Position based in Dublin, served by many transport links with flexibility for a blend of home and office working. * Dynamic and exciting industry and work environment. If you would like to apply for this role, please email a short cover letter along with your CV to our Product Manager, Francesca Rizzarello at francesca@traveldepartment.ie The closing date for applications is 28/05/2026\. Only applicants who are selected for interview will be contacted. Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * Sick pay Application question(s): * What are your salary expectations for this role? * Do you live in Dublin / within a close commute of Dublin? Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Vending Machine Merchandiser & Delivery Driver (Dublin)
description: BDS Vending Solutions, part of Coca\-Cola HBC group, is expanding and looking for reliable, customer\-focused drivers to join our Dublin team! This role combines Delivery Driving \& Vending Machine Merchandising, giving you a varied workday across customer sites nationwide. We provide full training, set shift pattern, and the opportunity to build a long\-term career with a growing company. **Available Shifts:** We currently have several full\-time shift options (40 or 45 hours per week): * **Monday – Friday:** 4am\-1:30pm * **Monday – Friday:** 6:30am\-3pm * **Tuesday \- Saturday:** 6:30am\-3pm * **Sunday \- Thursday:** 6:30am\-3pm Overtime opportunities are also available! **Work Location:** On the road **Head Office Location:** Parkwest, Dublin 12 **Pay:** Starting Annual Salary **€32,219\.20 to €36,246\.60** (depending on hours worked) **guaranteed increase to €33,185\.78 to €37,334 after 6mths** (upon completion of probation). **Job Type:** Full\-time \| Permanent **What You’ll Do** * Deliver stock to customer locations and team members across Ireland * Restock, clean, merchandise, and rotate products in vending machines * Maintain equipment and ensure machines are operating correctly * Provide excellent customer service at every site * Report machine issues or stock shortages to our support team * Maintain a professional appearance and safe working practices **What We’re Looking For** * Full, clean B Manual Driving Licence (required) * A customer\-focused attitude * Comfortable driving a Van and lifting shallow crates of snacks and drinks (weighting max 17kg) * Good conversational English and communication skills * Strong time management and reliability * Motivation to work independently and deliver excellent results No prior vending or delivery experience? No problem. Full training will be provided. **What we Offer** * Fulltime hours * Regular set shift * Permanent position with job security * 20 days annual leave * Full uniform and PPE provided * Comprehensive training * Employee Assistance Programme * Opportunities for overtime and career development * A supportive and inclusive workplace culture Pay: €32,219\.20\-€36,246\.60 per year Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * Free or subsidised travel * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Veterinary Practitioner - Small Animal - FTC (3 months)
description: **Veterinary Surgeon \- Small Animal** **Ark Vetcare, Dun Laoghaire Full\-time \| Fixed Term Contract Salary up to €61,500 DOE (FTE) Start Date: June 2026** **A Well\-Rounded GP Role with Real Support** We’re looking for a Small Animal Veterinary Surgeon to join our team in Dun Laoghaire for a 3 month fixed\-term contract (from June to September. This is a genuine general practice role, offering a balanced mix of consulting, medical case work\-ups, and routine surgery. You’ll be joining a supportive, collaborative environment where there’s always someone to discuss cases with, while still having the autonomy to manage your own day. It’s a great opportunity for a vet who enjoys variety and wants to continue building confidence in both medicine and surgery. **Working Pattern That Supports Balance** This is a full\-time position across five days per week, with a predictable and manageable rota. Typical hours are 7:30am to 4:30pm, with one later evening shift each week. Saturdays are worked on a 1 in 2 basis (8:00am\-2:00pm), with a half day off in lieu the following week. There’s no out\-of\-hours or Sunday work, and just one bank holiday per year (9:00am\-3:00pm), making this a role that genuinely supports a healthy work\-life balance. **What We’re Looking For** We’re seeking a VCI\-registered Veterinary Surgeon competent in first opinion practice. You’ll need to be confident in consultations, capable of working up medical cases, and comfortable with routine surgical procedures. Just as important is your attitude \- we’re looking for someone who is a team player, communicates well, and brings a positive approach to their work. This role isn’t suited to a new graduate, as a level of independence in clinical decision\-making is required. **About Ark Vetcare Dun Laoghaire** Ark Vetcare is a well\-established veterinary group with three practices across South County Dublin. Our Dun Laoghaire clinic has been part of the community for over 50 years and has built a strong reputation for high\-quality care and long\-standing client relationships. While the building itself reflects its history, the clinical standards are modern and progressive. You’ll be working with a diverse and engaged client base who value the care we provide. The team in Dun Laoghaire includes six Veterinary Surgeons, four Veterinary Nurses, an Animal Care Assistant, and five client care team members. There’s a great mix of experience levels, and two certificate holders who are actively involved in mentoring and case collaboration. It’s a genuinely supportive practice where people enjoy working together, and where work\-life balance is taken seriously. **Benefits \- Subject To Pro\-Rata** We offer a competitive salary, along with a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your wellbeing, development, and long\-term career. **Work Life Balance** 22 days Annual Leave Birthday Leave (paid day off on your birthday) **Focus on Employee Wellbeing** Paid Maternity Leave Package Paid Paternity Leave Cycle to Work Scheme Full access to EAP and Wellbeing Centre Paid Bereavement Leave Company Sick Pay **Development** €1300 annual CPD allowance with 5 days paid CPD days pro rata Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue Career Progression Opportunities, both clinical and non\-clinical **Professional Memberships** VCI membership paid VDS cover paid **Additional Benefits** Annual Pay Reviews Dedicated CARE fund \- annual contribution of €1,100 from IVC Evidensia for each Vet to use on a case of your choice to ensure successful outcome with your patient (T\&C’s apply) Pawesome Benefits \- reward gateway giving discounts and cashback with hundreds of retailers Employer Pension Contributions Free Flu Inoculations Discounted Veterinary Fees Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQ\+, Wellbeing Champions, Nurses Committee, Clinical Board and many more) **A Practice Where You Can Settle In and Grow** This is a great opportunity to join a well\-run, friendly practice where you’ll feel supported, valued, and part of the team from day one. If you’re looking for a solid GP role with variety, balance, and a genuinely positive working environment, we’d love to hear from you. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Up to €61,500 DOE
